The next part in the sequence on Laplace Transforms is now up. In case you missed it, here is the previous video [ https://substack.com/redirect/2d00fe33-404d-443a-bae7-ad61103d72c6?j=eyJ1IjoiOG81NG4ifQ.6oeetudtJEh-zO1onUJvtadWEdbodFIw0h4xbugrh1o ] introducing this transform, and here is the one before [ https://substack.com/redirect/4d0c25f6-c500-45ea-8ab8-8a87f1b47209?j=eyJ1IjoiOG81NG4ifQ.6oeetudtJEh-zO1onUJvtadWEdbodFIw0h4xbugrh1o ] laying down groundwork and intuition about complex exponents.
In this new part, we dig into a concrete example to see why this transform is useful both to understand the qualitative dynamics of a system and to find a final analytic solution.
